title: Renewable ketone waxes with unique carbon chain lengths and polarities
abstract: The present disclosure provides ketone waxes, methods of forming ketone waxes, and compositions comprising ketone waxes. In at least one embodiment, a ketone wax is provided. The ketone wax includes about 50 wt% or greater C40-C90 ketone content; about 50 wt% or greater of the ketone wax has a boiling point of 961Â°F or greater; and a paraffins content of less than about 10 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. In at least one embodiment, a method for forming a C40-C90 ketone wax includes exposing a feed stock to a basic catalyst under conditions suitable for coupling unsaturated carbon chains from the feed to form a composition including a ketone wax, oligomerizing the ketone wax to form a ketone wax having C40-C90 ketone wax, and distilling and/or extracting the oligomerized ketone wax to provide a C40-C90 ketone wax of the present disclosure.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A ketone wax comprising: about 50 wt% or greater C40-C90 ketone content; about 50 wt% or greater of the ketone wax has a boiling point of 961Â°F or greater; and a paraffins content of less than about 10 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 2. The ketone wax of claim 1, wherein the ketone wax has a IR-naphthene equivalent of from about 0.1 wt% to about 20 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 3. The ketone wax of claim 1 or 2, wherein the ketone wax has a IR-naphthene equivalent of from about 1 wt% to about 10 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 4. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the ketone wax has a paraffins content of from about 0 wt% to about 5 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 5. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the ketone wax has a 2R-naphthene equivalent of from about 0.1 wt% to about 20 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 6. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the ketone wax has a 2R-naphthene equivalent of from about 5 wt% to about 10 wt%, as determined by 2-dimensional gas chromatography. 7. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the ketone wax has a lR-aromatic equivalent of from about 50 wt% to about 99.9 wt%. 8. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the ketone wax has a lR-aromatic equivalent of from about 80 wt% to about 90 wt%. 9. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the ketone wax has a double bond equivalent value from 1 to about 6. 10. The ketone wax of claim 9, wherein less than about 30 wt% of the ketone wax has a double bond equivalent value of 6. 11. The ketone wax of claim 9 or 10, wherein less than about 20 wt% of the ketone wax has a double bond equivalent value of 5. 12. The ketone wax of any one of claims 9-11, wherein less than about 20 wt% of the ketone wax has a double bond equivalent value of 4. 13. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the ketone wax has an oxygen content of from about 1 wt% to about 15 wt%. 14. The ketone wax of claim 13, wherein the ketone wax has an oxygen content of from about 4 wt% to about 8 wt%. 15. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1 -14, wherein the ketone wax has about 80 wt% or greater C40-C90 ketone content. 16. The ketone wax of claim 15, wherein the ketone wax has about 95 wt% or greater C40-C90 ketone content. 17. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1-16, wherein about 95 wt% or greater of the ketone wax has a boiling point of 961Â°F or greater. 18. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1 -17, wherein the ketone wax has a kinematic viscosity at 100Â°C of from about 3.0 cSt to about 7.5 cSt. 19. The ketone wax of any one of claims 1 -18, wherein the ketone wax has a melting point of about 78Â°C or greater, as determined by differential scanning calorimetry. 20. A candle, coating, or personal care product comprising the ketone wax of any of claims 1 - 19. 21. A method for forming a ketone wax, said method comprising the steps of: a) exposing a feed stock to a basic catalyst under conditions suitable for coupling unsaturated carbon chains from the feed to form a composition including a ketone wax; b) oligomerizing the ketone wax over an acidic catalyst under conditions suitable to form a ketone wax having C40-C90 ketone wax; and c) distilling and/or extracting the oligomerized ketone wax to provide a C40-C90 ketone wax of the present disclosure. 22. The method of claim 21 wherein the basic catalyst of step a) comprises at least 5 wt% of a rare earth metal salt, alkali metal salt, or alkaline earth metal salt, relative to the total catalyst weight. 23. The method of claim 21 or 22 wherein the conditions of step a) comprise a temperature of about 300Â°C to about 450Â°C and a hydrogen partial pressure of about 1.8 MPag to about 35 MPag. 24. The method of any one of claims 21 -23 wherein the acidic catalyst of step b) comprises a molecular sieve, metalloaluminophosphate, amorphous aluminosilicate, cationic acidic clay, or a mixture thereof. 25. The method of any one of claims 21-24 wherein the conditions of step b) comprise a temperature of about 150Â°C to about 450Â°C, a liquid hourly space velocity of about 0.1 v/v/hour to about 10 v/v/hour, and a hydrogen partial pressure of about 1.8 MPag to about 35 MPag. 26. The method of any one of claims 21 -25 wherein steps a) and b) are performed concurrently in the same reaction environment.
